response.justification: The candidate is a seasoned, versatile engineer with a genuine developer-tooling bent — he shipped C/C++ components for the LabWindows/CVI IDE and has authored test runners and language libraries — but his core stack is TypeScript/Node.js and DLang, not the JVM. The role's hard requirements of strong Java and Kotlin expertise, deep JVM understanding, and solid Spring ecosystem experience are essentially absent: Java appears only as a secondary skill, and Kotlin and Spring do not appear at all. Despite clear seniority and craftsmanship, the stack mismatch is too large for a role centered on enterprise JVM tooling.
strengths: Direct IDE product experience building C/C++ components for NI LabWindows/CVI
Authentic language-tooling and DX passion (test runners, assertion libraries, web server libs, workshops)
Proven work on large-scale, performance-critical systems (millions of requests/sec at Sociomantic; Stripe/Shopify tenure)
concerns: No Kotlin and no Spring/enterprise-framework experience, both listed as required qualifications
Java exposure is minimal and dated; primary depth is in TypeScript/Node.js and DLang, not the JVM
IDE experience is brief (~7 months), in C/C++, and from 2014-2015 rather than recent JVM tooling work

key_factors.factor: IDE internals and developer-tooling track record
